validation_thesis: The opportunity should remain no_go unless the user can create a credible path to buyer access and protect enough recurring time to run a real validation motion. In 7 days, the test is whether the user can secure 5+ qualified conversations, 4+ hours/week of protected time, and at least 2 concrete next-step commitments or 1 repeatable channel source.
key_unknowns: Buyer-access risk: can the user reach qualified buyers without existing distribution?
Time-allocation risk: can the user protect at least 4 hours per week for the next 4 weeks?
Edge-creation risk: can the user produce one narrow credibility wedge that buyers respond to?
motion: Constraint-change plan using protected time, one credibility asset, targeted outreach, and concrete follow-up commitments.
day_by_day_plan: Day 1: choose one buyer and workflow, block 4 hours/week for 4 weeks, build a list of 20 prospects and 5 connectors/channel sources.
Day 2: create one credibility asset such as a teardown, checklist, benchmark, or audit template tied to the target workflow.
Day 3: send 20 targeted outreach messages or 5 intro asks plus 10 direct messages, offering a 15-minute call or custom teardown.
Day 4: complete the first 2-3 qualified conversations and ask for the strongest feasible next-step commitment.
Day 5: continue until 5 qualified conversations are completed or clearly unattainable, then refine the wedge and messaging.
Day 6: ask best prospects for a scheduled review, requested teardown, operator intro, or pilot-scope conversation; ask one source for repeatable channel access.
Day 7: review the evidence and decide go, revise, or kill.
success_criteria: Go: 4+ hours/week protected for the next 4 weeks.
Go: at least 2 concrete next-step commitments from prospects or 1 repeatable channel source.
Revise: 3-4 qualified conversations, protected time exists, and at least 1 concrete next step is earned.
kill_criteria: Kill: fewer than 3 qualified conversations after 20 targeted outreach attempts and 5 intro/channel asks.
Kill: no recurring 4 hours/week can be protected.
Kill: no prospect agrees to any concrete next step.
Kill: all interest points toward custom work outside a repeatable wedge.
evidence_to_capture: Calendar screenshot showing protected weekly time.
Prospect list with buyer type and outreach status.
Copies of outreach messages and response rates.
Notes from qualified conversations including urgency and current workaround.
Proof of concrete next-step commitments such as booked follow-ups, teardown requests, or channel introductions.
